title: Understanding Projectile Motion
text: To solve this problem, we need to understand the motion of a projectile. The projectile follows a parabolic path due to the influence of gravity.

title: Components of Velocity
text: The initial velocity of the projectile can be broken down into horizontal and vertical components. Use trigonometric functions to find these components.

title: Horizontal Motion
text: The horizontal motion of the projectile is uniform, meaning it has a constant velocity. Use this to find the horizontal displacement at $$15$$ seconds.

title: Vertical Motion
text: The vertical motion is affected by gravity. Use the kinematic equations to find the vertical displacement at $$15$$ seconds.

title: Calculate Horizontal Displacement
text: Use the formula for horizontal displacement: $$horizontal_{velocity}$$ * time. Calculate the horizontal displacement at $$15$$ seconds.

title: Calculate Vertical Displacement
text: Use the formula for vertical displacement: $$initial_{vertical,velocity}$$ * time - $$0.5$$ * g * $${time}^2$$. Calculate the vertical displacement at $$15$$ seconds.

title: Calculate Total Displacement
text: Use the Pythagorean theorem to find the magnitude of the total displacement: sqrt(horizontal_displacement**2 + vertical_displacement**2).
